claudeMd: # Task: Add Version/Last-Updated Timestamp to UI You are adding a small version/timestamp element to the game UI in `index.html`. ## Codebase Pure static HTML+JS served by nginx. No npm, no build step. - `index.html` — Main page with Three.js importmap, overlay UI, and all CSS - `js/main.js` — Game loop and state management - `js/tracker.js` — MediaPipe head tilt detection (DO NOT MODIFY) - `js/physics.js` — Ball physics (DO NOT MODIFY) - `js/renderer.js` — Three.js scene (DO NOT MODIFY) - `Dockerfile` + `nginx.conf` — Docker build serving on port 8080 ## What to Implement Only modify `index.html`. Add a version/timestamp element to the bottom-right corner. ### 1. CSS (add inside existing ``) ```css #version-info { position: fixed; bottom: 8px; right: 12px; color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3); font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif; font-size: 0.7em; z-index: 5; pointer-events: none; user-select: none; } ``` ### 2. HTML (add before the `

checks: A small text element showing 'v1.0.0' and 'Last updated: 2026-03-20' (or similar version/date format) is visible in the bottom-right corner of the screen
The version text does not overlap with the score display (top-left), leaderboard button (top-right), game-over overlay, or any gameplay elements like the ball, track, obstacles, or coins
The text is small (noticeably smaller than the score display), muted/low-opacity (appears as faint light gray), and does not visually distract from the game
The version string is a single hardcoded line in index.html that a developer can easily locate and update
